steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the price surge had a "counter-intuitive effect" on demand. Counter-intuitive means the opposite of what you'd expect: rising prices normally reduce demand, but here they did something unexpected to it. The blank needs a word meaning to produce or bring about this unexpected demand.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "exploiting": means taking unfair advantage of something; this doesn't describe what the price increase did to demand. ✗
  • "engendering": means causing or giving rise to—precisely how the price surge produced the new wave of demand among buyers expecting further appreciation. ✓
  • "appraising": means assessing the value of something; a price increase cannot appraise demand. ✗
  • "monetizing": means converting something into money; this doesn't describe an effect on demand. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
